page: Amazon Web Services r8gn.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, providing 64 vCPUs. The architecture includes 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications consist of 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 36 MB L3 caches. Hardware-accelerated AES cryptography is supported, while x86-specific instruction sets like AVX and AMX are absent.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, relying entirely on network-attached EBS storage. To support high-throughput storage and network operations, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring ultra-low latency local scratch space, though the high-speed network interface mitigates external storage bottlenecks. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with cached read bandwidth reaching 7394.84 GB/sec and uncached read bandwidth at 479.59 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance is strong, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 109076.78 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 25460.0. Database and memory-bound operations perform exceptionally well, with Redis SET operations reaching 133.96 million operations per second and Passmark database operations scoring in the top tier. However, LLM text generation for small models is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the poor category at 26.8 tokens per second, whereas medium and large model text generation shows strong relative performance. The custom ARM64 architecture delivers high resource density and memory bandwidth, offering qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives.

The r8gn.16xlarge is designed for memory-intensive and network-bound workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale in-memory databases like Redis, high-throughput database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-to-large scale LLM inference tasks where GPU acceleration is not required.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz, it provides 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying instead on a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for EBS and network connectivity. Benchmarks show top-tier memory bandwidth, with cached reads at 7394.84 GB/sec, and exceptional database performance, including 133.96 million Redis SET operations per second. Multi-core processing is strong, though small-model LLM text generation is a performance bottleneck. This instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive deployments. It is suited for in-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gn.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated physical cores, massive memory bandwidth, and high-speed networking for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz.
512.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS and network throughput.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance, including Redis and Passmark benchmarks.
No local storage or GPU hardware included.
Best suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
